motto: Serving Greywater Falls since 1887, and the lake somewhat longer.
weather: Overcast and grey, 23°F. The sky has come down close, the way it does before snow that doesn't arrive. No wind. The cold is the perfectly still kind, the kind that sits in the lungs. The lake surface is white from shore to shore. The ice holds. By afternoon a flat light off the clouds that makes everything look slightly earlier than it is.

undercurrent.note: Agnes's letter is the third object-event: the blank return slip on Wednesday (received Saturday), the swept floor on Sunday, and now Agnes's public letter naming the clean floor as 'the right way to receive something' — which is the first time a resident has spoken, in print, about the alcove as if it is already in the business of receiving. Agnes did not say what it is receiving. She did not need to. The shelving is still on order. The door is still locked. But Agnes is now on record in the Gazette, which means the town has begun, quietly and collectively, to notice the thing this editor has been writing in the second notebook. Level ticks to 19: the thread has been seen. Stage holds at 0: it has not yet been named.

editor.name: Wren Aldercott
editor.voice: Dry, precise, allergic to drama, which is unfortunate given recent events. Writes like a person holding a clipboard while standing in rising water. Lets one true thing slip per edition, usually in the last line.

season_arc.premise: The library's east alcove has been empty since 2009, when the last cataloguer left in the middle of a Wednesday with no explanation. Calloway has begun shelving it. The books that appear on the new shelves overnight are not books the library owns, and they are not books that have ever been published, but they are about Greywater Falls — and they are set in years that have not happened yet.
